title: Tenant Rights Information/Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Tenant Rights Information/Counseling 19 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Domestic Violence Issues Mobile Home Residents No Filter Domestic Violence Issues Mobile Home Residents CITY OF CORVALLIS LIVABILITY CODE ENFORCEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts and handles complaints about housing conditions under the Corvallis Livability Code and coordinates investigation and code enforcement, as necessary. 501 SW Madison Avenue Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-11am and noon-5pm Email (541) 766-6944 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND HOUSING BUREAU RENTAL SERVICES HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers general information and technical assistance with Portland City Codes, which provide residential rental consumer protections, information about state and federal landlord/tenant laws, and/or recommend resources for additional information. The Rental Services Office cannot provide legal or business advice or intervene in a landlord/tenant dispute. They do not supply direct rent payments, relocation, or other financial assistance. 1900 SW Fourth Avenue Suite 7007 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-11am and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 823-1303 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND PERMITTING AND DEVELOPMENT PROPERTY COMPLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the City of Portland's minimum standards for property maintenance and safety for residential buildings. It enforces these standards through a program of inspections. Portland City Code lists a variety of minimum housing maintenance requirements. The inspector asks: -Are heat, water, and power safe and adequate? -Is the structure secure and stable? -Are there plumbing, electrical, or mechanical hazards? -Are the stairs, handrails, and guardrails safe and adequate? Also investigates abandoned and disabled vehicles, derelict buildings (vacant, boarded up), nuisance (in exterior property areas such as yards and driveways) which includes overgrown grass and weeds, trash and debris, scrap metal, auto parts, and appliances. In cases of serious mold in rental property, where the tenant has become ill as a result, an inspection may be done as part of an assessment. All building and safety regulations and concerns can be reported through here. 1900 SW 4th Avenue Suite 5000 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: -Person making complaint must provide contact information (remains confidential) -Property of concern must be within area served Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 823-2633 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ALLIANCE OF TENANTS RENTERS' RIGHTS HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a renters' rights hotline in Oregon. Topics include fair housing protections, evictions, terminations, repair concerns, security deposits, and rent increase limits, etc. Does not provide information for landlords. Priority is given to renters in Southern and Central Oregon, Mid-Valley, and Portland/Tri-County area. Residents of all other Oregon regions strongly encouraged to become members to receive prioritized assistance. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Hotline intake and callbacks: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday 1pm-5pm, Tuesday 5pm-8pm Member's Live Hotline: Tuesday 6pm-7pm, Saturday 1pm-2pm Email (503) 288-0130 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about tenant rights and refers cases requiring legal advice or representation to attorneys at Legal Aid Services of Oregon, Fair Housing Council of Oregon, and Oregon Law Center. Also includes culturally-specific case workers located in geographically underserved areas. Also conducts outreach and tenant rights education in the community. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and representation on domestic abuse and family law matters. Information available in regard to immigration, public benefits, tenant/landlord disputes, discrimination law, and contested restraining orders. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assault Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, referrals, and support for landlord-tenant issues, fair housing, and complaints of housing discrimination. Maintains listings of low-income housing in Clackamas County, including non-subsidized low-cost apartments. Manages Housing Authority-owned units, subsidized rental housing, and the Section 8/Housing Choice Voucher program. Handles complaints related to public housing and Section 8. 13930 S Gain Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Varies by program or property Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, holiday hours posted on website Email (503) 655-8267 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES MANUFACTURED AND MARINA COMMUNITIES RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides technical assistance and dispute resolution services to landlords and tenants in manufactured dwelling parks. Provides copies of relevant laws and general information on rights and responsibilities. Assists clients in making decisions about how to handle manufactured dwelling park-related situations. May be referred to local mediation services for dispute resolution. Also maintains the statewide Manufactured Home Park Directory, which offers information on spaces available for rent. Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (800) 453-5511 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER COOS BAY REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 490 N 2nd Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Walk in: Monday/Wednesday 1:30pm-4pm Telephone hours only: Tuesday/Thursday 1:30pm-4pm (541) 269-1226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER GRANTS PASS REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Offers a senior law hotline. Some locations may offer additional services. 424 NW 6th Street Suite 102 Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 1pm-4pm (541) 471-3033 1-4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER HILLSBORO REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family (focused on domestic violence), stalking, child support, employment, or elder law. Offers a tenant advice hotline. 230 NE Second Avenue Suite F Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 2pm-5pm, Friday 9am-noon; closed on 4th Friday of the month Tenant Hotline: Monday/Wednesday 9am-noon (503) 640-4115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER LANE COUNTY LEGAL AID GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides general legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 101 E Broadway Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Office hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-11:45am and 12:45pm-5pm Intake for new clients: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 1pm-3pm (541) 485-1017 100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER MCMINNVILLE OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family (focused on domestic violence), stalking, child support, employment, or elder law. Offers a tenant advice hotline. 117 NE 5th Street Suite B McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Closed the 2nd Monday and 4th Wednesday of the month (503) 472-9561 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Website offers legal information regarding various issues, including housing, family law, employment law, elder law, government benefits, and consumer protection. Provides links to legal service providers, resources, and a directory of legal aid providers. 621 SW Morrison Street Suite 1450 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (503) 224-4086 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER SALEM REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Offers a tenant advocate hotline. Some locations may offer additional services. 280 Liberty Street Suite 200 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-6pm, Friday 9am-noon (503) 485-0696 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER ST HELENS OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family (focused on domestic violence), stalking, child support, employment, or elder law. Offers a tenant advice hotline. 270 S 1st Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday/Tuesday 9:30am-2:30pm, Wednesday 9:30am-noon, Thursday 9:30am-noon and 12:30pm-2:30pm (503) 397-1628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPRINGFIELD EUGENE TENANT ASSOCIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hotline/message phone to tenants experiencing issues with housing or landlord disputes who need information about renters' rights. Also offers online resources about evictions and terminations, discrimination and retaliation, habitability and repairs, and guides about leases, deposits, and fees. Eligibility: Individuals experiencing tenancy issues with rental properties Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 972-3715 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about tenant rights, eviction prevention, and legal assistance, referring cases requiring legal advice or representation by attorneys to Legal Aid Services of Oregon, Fair Housing Council of Oregon, and Oregon Law Center. Also, conducts outreach and tenant rights education in the community. Also includes culturally-specific case workers located in geographically underserved areas. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON HOME FREE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual intervention program that includes services such as: -Emergency Services: Hotline, information and referral, and telephone advocacy -Housing Services: Home visits, long-term advocacy and support, help to locate and maintain safe housing -Domestic and sexual violence healthcare and advocacy -Court and legal advocacy with a restraining order hotline -Economic Empowerment: Credit building and repair advocacy, savings, interview skills, employment seekers group, barrier reduction assistance, and Rent Well landlord-tenant education Motel vouchers are only available for those in emergencies who are actively fleeing domestic violence. Availability is dependent upon funding. Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Emergency Hotline: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Restraining Order Services: Monday-Friday 8am-2pm Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 235-8655 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF CORVALLIS LIVABILITY CODE ENFORCEMENT CITY OF PORTLAND RENTAL SERVICES HELPLINE CITY OF PORTLAND PROPERTY COMPLIANCE EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE HOUSING AUTHORITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE
